channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pfizer’s experimental drug atirmociclib shows promising results in controlling breast cancer progression
item.description: Pfizer has announced promising results from a mid-stage clinical trial of its experimental drug, atirmociclib, in combination with fulvestrant for breast cancer patients. The trial focused on postmeno...

item.title: Malawian High Commissioner Bids Farewell After Completing Tenure in Tanzania
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, Deputy Minister for Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation, Ngwaru Maghembe, held a farewell meeting with the outgoing Malawian High Commissioner, Andrew Kumwenda, at the Minis...

item.title: Sindh Forms Panel to Update Christian Marriage and Divorce Laws
item.description: The Sindh government has set up a three-member committee to examine and propose amendments to the Christian Marriage Act of 1872 and the Divorce Law of 1869, both dating back to British colonial times...

item.title: Comparison of Pakistan and Afghan Taliban Military Capabilities Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Recent escalations between Pakistan and Afghanistan have brought military capabilities of both sides into sharp focus. Pakistani authorities, responding to unprovoked attacks by the Afghan Taliban, ca...

item.title: Seven Individuals Sentenced for Multiple Luxury Store Burglaries in London
item.description: Seven men have received prison sentences for their involvement in a series of violent smash-and-grab burglaries at high-end shops across London between May and July 2025. The Metropolitan Police repor...

item.title: Drivers Manage P5,000 Government Aid Amid Rising Fuel Prices
item.description: Drivers in Metro Manila are feeling the pinch as rising oil prices continue to strain their daily budgets. The government recently provided cash assistance of P5,000 to help drivers cope with the incr...

item.title: Law Commission proposes reforms to allow reuse of century-old graves amid burial space shortage
item.description: The Law Commission has proposed reforms that would allow the reuse of graves more than 100 years old in cemeteries across England and Wales in response to a growing shortage of burial space. The refor...

item.title: Rulani Mokwena explains airport cash incident behind Algeria detention
item.description: Former Mamelodi Sundowns and Orlando Pirates coach Rulani Mokwena has addressed the circumstances that led to his detention at an airport in Algeria, where he was reportedly found with a large amount ...

item.title: Maika Hamano scores as Japan maintains advantage in tight match
item.description: Japan managed to extend their lead in a closely contested soccer match, thanks to a precise goal by Maika Hamano. The forward struck from a challenging angle, demonstrating excellent skill under press...

item.title: ADB Launches 5-Year Strategy to Support Pakistan’s Economic Growth and Private Sector Development
item.description: The Asian Development Bank (ADB) has announced plans to provide around $10 billion in financing to Pakistan over the next five years through its 2026-30 Country Partnership Strategy (CPS2026-30). The ...

item.title: Kerri Einarson secures fifth consecutive win at Women's World Curling Championship
item.description: Canada’s Kerri Einarson continues to demonstrate her dominance in the world of curling with her fifth straight win at the Women's World Curling Championship. Einarson, skip of the Canadian team, has c...

item.title: Astral Foods expects significant earnings growth amid strong poultry demand
item.description: South Africa’s largest poultry producer, Astral Foods, is anticipating a substantial increase in its earnings for the first half of the year, with headline earnings per share (HEPS) projected to rise ...

item.title: Cyclone Narelle set to impact far north Queensland with destructive winds and heavy rain
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is rapidly intensifying as it approaches far north Queensland, forecast to cross the coast between Cooktown and Lockhart River early Thursday. Already the third system to affe...

item.title: NPFL to Observe Minute’s Silence for Henry Nwosu Across All Matches
item.description: The Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) don direct say all Matchday 30 fixtures go hold one minute silence in honour of former Green Eagles captain, Henry Nwosu, wey don pass away at 62. NPFL chair...

item.title: Edmonton Oilers overcome Sharks 5-3 without Draisaitl
item.description: The Edmonton Oilers demonstrated strong team cohesion in their 5-3 victory over the San Jose Sharks on March 17, 2026, despite the absence of star forward Leon Draisaitl, who will miss the rest of the...

item.title: Mumbai Family Grapples with Son's Vegetative State, Rejects Euthanasia Parallels to Harish Rana Case
item.description: In Mumbai, the family of 35-year-old Anand Dixit is facing a heartbreaking situation after he has been in a persistent vegetative state for over two and a half years following a serious accident. Anan...

item.title: Former Bloc Québécois MP Pascal-Pierre Paillé charged with sexual offences against two minors
item.description: Pascal-Pierre Paillé, a 47-year-old former Bloc Québécois Member of Parliament for the Louis-Hébert riding from 2008 to 2011 and current teacher, has been charged with multiple sexual offences against...

item.title: Court Incident: Sowore’s Lawyer Refuses Judge’s Unlawful Order to Kneel
item.description: On Monday for Federal High Court, drama burst when Justice Mohammed Umar tell lawyer for activist Omoyele Sowore make e kneel inside court. The lawyer, Marshal Abubakar, sharply reject the order, talk...

item.title: Boko Haram Demand Huge Ransom for 176 Kidnapped Residents in Kwara State
item.description: Boko Haram don abduct 176 people from Woro community for Kwara State, and dem dey demand huge sum of ₦3.52 billion for their release. Families and community leaders don dey try negotiate, but talks ne...

item.title: DILG Urges LGUs to Reduce Electricity and Fuel Consumption Amid Rising Costs
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has directed all local government units (LGUs) nationwide to reduce their electricity and fuel consumption by 10% to 20%. This initiative fol...
